archetype_reasoning: This trader covers 52 distinct geo markets across multiple domains (Trump_US, Canada, Germany, Iran, LatAm, Europe), demonstrating genuine breadth. Directionality of 1.0 indicates they always trade one side rather than LP behaviour, and many entries occur at genuinely contested prices (0.17-0.67). However, the 54% accuracy across 157 resolved trades is barely above chance, and recent resolved history shows a persistent string of losses, raising questions about genuine edge versus noise.
domain_blindspots: LatAm: 0/1 accuracy, low sample but no demonstrated edge
Europe: 0/1 accuracy, near-certainty entries observed (AfD 0.93) suggest overconfidence
domain_summary: Marginal edge in Trump_US and Other (both ~54%) but insufficient to be confident this is skill over variance given sample size. No demonstrated edge outside those two domains; Europe and Russia_UKR trades show poor calibration and some near-certainty entries that went wrong.
signal_weight_reasoning: Active ELO of 2136 is near-legendary but below the 2175 threshold. Win rates across all domains hover near 50-55%, barely distinguishable from noise. Breadth is real but accuracy is marginal. Some near-certainty entries (Trump Texas 0.95, Liberal Party margin 0.96, AfD 0.93) that lost suggest calibration problems. Signal is worth tracking but should not be given full weight without corroboration.
behavioural_flags: Directionality of 1.0 — never trades the opposing side, suggesting strong conviction bias rather than probabilistic hedging
Near-certainty entries: Trump Texas at 0.95 YES, Liberal majority at 0.986 NO, AfD 20-25% at 0.93 YES — multiple large-price entries that resolved against
Clustering of losses in bulk-resolved batch (2026-06-04) suggests legacy positions from 2024 US election cycle that underperformed
Open positions include several very-low-probability YES bets (Liberal 3-6% win margin at 0.016, Minnesota Republican Senate at 0.034) suggesting a longshot/lottery ticket pattern
Reasonable position sizing discipline — shares range widely but no single catastrophic outlier visible
risk_patterns: Persistent near-certainty buying on contested outcomes (e.g., AfD, Trump Texas, Liberal margin) indicates overconfidence or yield-harvesting behaviour creeping into otherwise genuine forecasting
Longshot accumulation in open positions (very low prices, high share counts) could indicate sunk-cost doubling-down on wrong-side theses
15 consecutive losses in recent resolved history is a significant red flag — unclear if this is a bad run or systematic deterioration
Concentration in US electoral markets with thin edge could mean realized PnL is largely driven by a few big wins masking poor base accuracy

open_positions_summary: Current open book is dominated by longshot YES positions: very low-probability Canadian election margin bets (Liberal 3-6% at 0.016, Conservative 150-159 seats), a Peruvian election candidate (Carlos Álvarez, dual bets), Kharg Island control by April 30 (0.04), and several US Republican longshots (Minnesota Senate, New Mexico presidential, NJ Senate). The overall thesis implies this trader is systematically accumulating low-probability YES positions for outsized returns — a longshot portfolio strategy. Also holds a Catherine Connolly Irish Presidential bet at 0.20 showing some international breadth.
watch_items: Investigate whether multiple 'LOST' resolutions on outcomes that appear to have gone the trader's way (Carney PM, Republican OH Senate, R sweep) are data errors or genuine losses from wrong market framing
Monitor whether the longshot open positions (sub-0.10 entries) resolve positively — a few hits could significantly change PnL and ELO trajectory
Watch for any new entries in Trump_US domain in 2026 midterm cycle as this is the primary domain
Track if losing streak continues into 2026 markets — 15 consecutive losses is a material signal about current form
Monitor Peruvian election positions closely as LatAm is a stated blindspot
summary: This trader has genuine breadth across 52 geo markets and a near-legendary ELO, but the underlying accuracy (~54%) is marginally above chance and a long recent losing streak raises questions about current calibration. Several notable resolved-as-LOST positions appear to be on outcomes that went the trader's way, suggesting possible data quality issues that — if corrected — could significantly improve the apparent track record. Open positions reveal a systematic longshot accumulation strategy at very low prices, which is speculative and distinct from the contested-price genuine forecasting seen in some earlier trades. Treat as a PARTIAL signal source with higher trust in Trump_US domain calls and caution on all others.
